content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

April 10, 1991


Loretta H. Roper
City Clerk, City of Santee
10765 Woodside Avenue
Santee, CA 92071-3198

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. I-91-151

Dear Ms. Roper:

You have requested assistance in determining if the Santee
Chamber of Commerce Political Action Committee ("SANPAC") has
fully complied with the campaign disclosure requirements of the
Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/

The following information will be helpful to you in
determining if SANPAC has complied with the requirements of the
Act.

A general purpose recipient committee must file campaign
disclosure statements (Form 420 or 450) at least two each
calendar year. These "semi-annual" statements are due in July and
January each year. During a six-month period in which a city
holds an election, a city general purpose recipient committee must
also file pre-election statements if the committee makes
contributions or independent expenditures totaling $500 or more
during the period covered by the pre-election statement. A
recipient committee that makes independent expenditures does not
also become an independent expenditure committee. The committee
must disclose the independent expenditures on the next Form 420
filed.

In addition, the Supplemental Independent Expenditure Report
(Form 465) is required to be filed by recipient committees, major
donor committees and independent expenditure committees which make
independent expenditures totaling $500 or more in a calendar year
to support or oppose a single candidate, a single measure, or the
qualification of a single measure. A major Form 465 must be

1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Loretta H. Roper
Page Two

filed for each candidate or measure being supported or opposed and
must be filed at the same times and with each filing officer with
whom the candidate or a committee supporting or opposing the
measure is required to file campaign statements. (See the
enclosed Form 465 and instructions.)

As a filing officer, you must fully review all original
campaign disclosure statements required to be filed with you.
Enclosed is a copy of the Handbook for City and County Filing
Officers which outlines the duties of filing officers. Contained
in the Handbook is Regulation 18110, which identifies the specific
items that filing officers must review on campaign disclosure
statements. (See Appendix B.)

Although it is not your obligation to perform any audit or
investigation to determine if the information provided on a
campaign disclosure statement is accurate, you should notify
persons filing campaign statements with you if you believe that
information has been inadvertently omitted, or that there is some
misunderstanding of the Act's requirements. For example, it
appears from the materials you have provided that some of SANPAC's
expenditures may have been contributions to or independent
expenditures to support candidates in the city's most recent
election.

The fact that none of the expenditures listed on the
committee's campaign statements are identified as contributions or
independent expenditures may mean there is some misunderstanding
on the part of the committee concerning the Act's definitions and
reporting requirements. In addition, it is possible that the
committee needs some assistance concerning the identification
requirements for mass mailings. You may wish to contact the
committee, express your concerns, and recommend that they contact
the Commission for assistance.

A member of our staff will contact the committee concerning
its use of a computer-generated campaign statement.

Please call me at (916) 322-5662 if you have any questions
regarding this matter.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
Acting General Counsel

By: Alice Hughes
Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure
